title: Human Longevity Inc. and LEV Foundation Partner to Study Centenarian and Supercentenarian Blood
description: HLI and LEV Foundation launch a collaboration to decode biological drivers of exceptional longevity through multi-omic analysis of blood from individuals aged 100+ and 110+, with Aubrey de Grey joining HLI's advisory board.

title: UT Health San Antonio Launches NIA-Funded Rapamycin Dosing and Safety Clinical Trial
description: A new NIA-funded randomized placebo-controlled trial of ~84 older adults will evaluate rapamycin dosing, safety, and biological markers to establish evidence-based guidance for the drug's use in healthy aging.

title: Inside the Longevity Boom: Where Science Ends and Fantasy Begins
description: The longevity sector attracted $8.49B in 2024 (a 220% jump) and is projected to reach $8T by 2030 per UBS, while USC researcher Valter Longo warns people are living longer but sicker.

title: Bryan Johnson's 2026 Blueprint Update: Drops Rapamycin, Adds Lithium and NDGA
description: Bryan Johnson's updated 2026 supplement stack takes NMN/NR six days a week, drops rapamycin entirely, and adds low-dose lithium and NDGA for brain function and cellular support.

title: NIA ITP March 2026 Results: Zero New Compounds Extended Lifespan
description: The NIH Interventions Testing Program's March 2026 results showed zero new compounds extended lifespan; pioglitazone shortened female lifespan by 6.2% after 20 years of testing.

title: Longevity Investing in 2026: A Strategic Guide for Family Offices
description: The longevity biotech market valued at ~$30.7B in 2026 is projected to reach $52.8B by 2035 at 6.5% CAGR, driven by epigenetic reprogramming, senolytics, and organ regeneration.

title: China Approves Stem Cell and Gene Editing Regulation Effective May 1, 2026
description: China's new biomedical technologies regulation takes effect May 1, establishing a unified framework covering stem cells, iPSCs, CAR-T, and CRISPR -- a landmark moment for global regenerative medicine.

title: The Business of Longevity: LA Turns Aging into an Investable Market
description: Biotech labs, healthtech startups, and venture capital firms converge in Southern California around scalable healthtech, funding platforms combining diagnostics, AI, and recurring care models.

title: Men Are Losing the Y Chromosome with Age -- and It May Be Deadly
description: Aging men lose the Y chromosome in growing numbers of cells, linked to heart disease, cancer, Alzheimer's, and shorter lifespans, with new research showing the phenomenon is far more dangerous than once believed.

title: Popular Anti-Aging Compound Dasatinib+Quercetin Causes Brain Damage in Mice
description: UConn researchers report in PNAS that the senolytic combo D+Q causes callosal brain damage in mice at standard doses, urging caution for prophylactic prescribing.

title: Blueprint Protocol 2026 Update: Data-Driven Refinements and New Biomarkers
description: Bryan Johnson's 2026 Blueprint update emphasizes organ-specific metrics, real-time wearable data, and frequent lab panels, shifting from fixed rules to adaptive data-driven refinement.

title: Thymic Atrophy Degree Correlates with Mortality and Age-Related Disease
description: A large-scale study in Nature (n=25,031 NLST + n=2,581 FHS) finds that the degree of thymic atrophy is a strong predictor of mortality and age-related disease.

title: Your Daily Coffee May Be Protecting Your Brain, 43-Year Study Finds
description: A long-term study found moderate consumption of caffeinated coffee or tea linked to 18% lower risk of dementia and better cognitive performance over time.

title: The $45B Cell Therapy Boom: Avai Bio Manufactures Alpha-Klotho Longevity Protein Cells
description: Avai Bio initiates manufacturing of a Master Cell Bank of genetically modified cells that overexpress alpha-Klotho, a key longevity protein, as cell therapy manufacturing hits new milestones.

title: Longevity Biotech Market Projected to Reach $45.8B by 2033 at 20.4% CAGR
description: Valued at $12.5B in 2026, the longevity biotech market is projected to hit $45.8B by 2033, driven by AI-powered drug discovery, epigenetic clocks, and personalized longevity medicine.

title: Shingles Vaccine Drastically Cuts Risk of Serious Cardiac Events
description: A TriNetX database study of 246,822 individuals finds the shingles vaccine is associated with a large reduction in cardiac risk, alongside evidence it may slow biological aging.

title: Stanford's Anne Brunet Explores Promising Frontiers of Aging Research
description: Geneticist Anne Brunet discusses what aging really is, how lifestyle choices influence longevity, and the promising frontiers including epigenetic reprogramming and metabolic interventions.

title: Vitamin C Inhibits ACSL4 to Alleviate Ferro-Aging in Primates
description: Steve Horvath highlights March 2026 as a strong month for everyday supplement research, pointing to a new primate study on vitamin C's role in combating ferroptosis-driven aging.

title: Shanghai Biotech Unixell Wins FDA Clearance for First Stem Cell Epilepsy Therapy
description: For the first time, the FDA clears an allogeneic stem cell therapy for epilepsy to begin human testing, marking a milestone for China-based cell therapy development.

title: The Senolytics Pipeline in 2026: Where the Science Still Looks Strong
description: A 2026 Nature Aging study compared 21 senolytic agents, finding BCL-2 inhibitor ABT263 and BET inhibitor ARV825 most effective; the field is narrowing toward biomarker-guided precision approaches.

title: Top Longevity Startups by Fundraising: $6.9B Across Top Five Alone
description: Altos Labs ($3B), Oura ($1.2B), Retro Biosciences ($1.2B), BioSplice ($778M), and Insilico Medicine lead longevity funding, with the top 10 capturing ~67% of total sector investment.

title: Daily Multivitamins Slow Biological Aging in Two-Year Clinical Trial
description: Published in Nature Medicine, a two-year trial found multivitamins slowed all five biological aging clocks by about four months, with the most benefit in those who were biologically older at baseline.

title: ARPA-H Devotes $144M to First Major Clinical Trials Targeting Aging
description: The PROSPR program awards seven teams up to $144M to lay groundwork for phase 3 trials of aging interventions including rapamycin, semaglutide, and SGLT2 inhibitors in healthy 60-somethings.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Mitochondrial Stress Key to Senolytics
description: Monthly roundup highlights Nature Aging findings on mitochondrial stress driving senolytic effectiveness, restoring NK cell function, and SIRT6 protecting against brain protein disorders.

title: Scientists Reveal Why Anti-Aging Compound Spermidine May Also Fuel Cancer
description: Polyamines like spermidine boost autophagy and healthy aging via eIF5A1, but a nearly identical protein eIF5A2 drives aggressive tumor growth, revealing a double-edged longevity mechanism.
